description:
You are an automation specialist who has decided to keep all personal-level service units in one place inside your home directory.  

Goal  
Create a new user-level systemd unit file named “workflow.service” and, in the same command line, record a confirmation log.  The whole job MUST be done with ONE single bash command (chaining with “&&”, a subshell or a here-document is fine, but it must be exactly one command line that you press <enter> on).

Required end-state of the filesystem  
1. Directory “/home/user/services” must exist (create it if it does not).  
2. File “/home/user/services/workflow.service” must exist and its **entire** contents must match **exactly** the block shown below, including the trailing newline after the last line:  

```
[Unit]
Description=Workflow Runner Service

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/bin/python3 /home/user/scripts/run_workflow.py
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

```  

3. File “/home/user/services/creation.log” must exist and contain exactly this single line (again note the trailing newline):  

```
workflow.service created successfully

```  

Constraints & notes  
• Achieve everything with ONE single terminal command line.  
• Do not rely on root privileges; stay completely inside /home/user.  
• You do NOT have to start or enable the unit with systemctl – only create the files and the log.  
• It’s fine if your command prints normal shell output; automated validation will only inspect the two files listed above.  
• Use a here-document or echo redirections—whatever you prefer—as long as it is all in a single command line.  
• If you decide to use a here-document, remember to quote it (<<'EOF') so that no shell variable expansion happens.  

The automated tests will check:  
• Existence of /home/user/services/workflow.service with byte-for-byte identical content to the block above.  
• Existence of /home/user/services/creation.log with the single expected line.  
• That both files contain a trailing newline character.
truth:
Expected directory tree and exact file contents after successful execution:

/home/user/services/                    (directory)
/home/user/services/workflow.service    (file, 8 lines + trailing newline)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
[Unit]
Description=Workflow Runner Service

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/bin/python3 /home/user/scripts/run_workflow.py
Restart=on-failure

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

/home/user/services/creation.log        (file, 1 line + trailing newline)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
workflow.service created successfully

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
